rulings.Case Title: INFORMATION DELIVERY SERVICES INC VS. ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 7, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, CRAIG WICHNER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action. Plaintiff must allege false representations with specificity, and representations must be statement of fact, not opinions or future predictions. Plaintiff must plead defendants acts with requisite intent. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to third cause of action, no authority that this claim recognized in California. No reasonabale probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Demurrer overruled as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT S. VAILE VS. DELOITTE & TOUCHE, LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Second Amended Motion And Second Amended Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And Subject Matter Jurisdiction; Alternatively Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 7, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT CISILIE PORSBOLL, MARSHAL WILLICK, RICHARD CRANE, MARSHAL S. WILLICK, P.C., A NEVADA PROFESSIONAL Notice Of Second Amended Motion And Second Amended Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And Subject Matter Jurisdiction; Alternatively Forum Non Conveniens. Motion denied in part and granted in part. Motion to quash denied. Defendants Willick and Willick P.C. subject to specific jurisdiction in California in that they instituted a wage garnishment procedure affecting a California resident and his earning within the state. However, this action is stayed pending resolution in Nevada on the grounds of forum non conveniens. The Nevada proceedings have been lengthly and ongoing, and the garnishment arose from a Nevada judgment. The witnesses, evidence, court files and parties are located in Nevada. The parties are subject to Nevada's jurisdiction. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT S. VAILE VS. DELOITTE & TOUCHE, LLP

rulings.Case Title: JAY MORTON VS. KATHLEEN M BARISH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Under The California Anti-Slapp Statute; Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16; Declarations Of Karl Olson And Kathleen M. Barish
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 7, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT KATHLEEN BARISH Motion To Strike Under The California Anti-Slapp Statute; Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16. Motion granted. Late opposition considered. "Reply to Reply" and "Supplement to Opposition" stricken as untimely. The lawsuit arises out of protected activity. (Complaint paragraphs 2 and 5.) CCP section 425.16 is broadly interpreted. Civil Code section 47 privilege applies to the alleged statements that have some relation to a judicial proceeding. The burden shifts to plaintiff who offers no legally admissible evidence demonstrating a probability of prevailing on the merits. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FEED BAG, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LILLIE A MOSADDEGH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 7, 2010, line 14, PLAINTIFF FEED BAG, INC.,MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion granted. Plaintiff shifts the burden and no triable issue of material fact raised. No evidence presented that defendant or Dr. Matthews gave the required written notice to exercise the second option. No dispute regarding damages that are based on a figure found in defendant's discovery responses. =(302/CWW)
